observation_text: He pointed at a red bird outside to show us what he wants.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Points to ask for something or get help

observation_text: Kuch lene ke liye ungli se ishaara kiya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Points to ask for something or get help

observation_text: She looked directly at her dog when I asked 'where is the dog?'.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Looks at familiar object when named

observation_text: Toy car bolne par car ki taraf dekhne lagta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Looks at familiar object when named

observation_text: He handed me his toy cup when I held out my hand and requested it.
Paas aao bolne par turant chal kar aa gaya.
She says three single words clearly: ball, cup, and bye-bye.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses several single words besides mama or dada

observation_text: Mama aur dada ke alawa car aur cat bolta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses several single words besides mama or dada

observation_text: She said 'more milk' when she pointed to her empty cup.
Papa aao bol kar haath khichne laga.
He pointed to the cow in the picture book when we asked.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Points to picture in book when asked

observation_text: Book me chidiya kahan hai bolne par chidiya dikhata hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Points to picture in book when asked

observation_text: She touched her nose and eyes when we played the naming game.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Points to at least two body parts

observation_text: Aankh aur kaan poochne par unhe touch karta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Points to at least two body parts

observation_text: He waved goodbye and said 'bye' when his aunt left.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses physical gestures along with words

observation_text: Haath hila kar tata bola jab hum ja rahe the.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses physical gestures along with words

observation_text: She spoke a sentence saying: 'me want big balloon'.
He said 'daddy go to office' in the morning.
She pointed to the sky and asked: 'what is that?'.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Asks basic who, what, where questions

observation_text: Pooch raha tha ki 'doggy kahan gaya?'.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Asks basic who, what, where questions

observation_text: He picked up the socks and put them in the laundry bin when asked.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Follows instructions with two related steps

observation_text: Chammach uthao aur plate me rakho bola to usne dono kaam kiye.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Follows instructions with two related steps

observation_text: She named the cat and key in the coloring book picture.
Kitab me apple aur car ko dekh kar bolta hai.
He kept asking 'why does it rain?' during the shower.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Asks why, how, and when questions

observation_text: Gadi kaise chalti hai aur hum kyun ja rahe hain poochta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Asks why, how, and when questions

observation_text: She spoke to the visitor saying: 'I want to play block game'.
Strangers can understand when she says 'my dog is very big'.
He told me a story about how he went to the store and bought juice.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Recounts a simple story or event

observation_text: She explained that she played with Eli on the slides in the park.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Recounts a simple story or event

observation_text: He correctly called the blocks green, red, and yellow.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Identifies colors and basic shapes by name

observation_text: Lal aur neela rang pehchan kar batata hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Identifies colors and basic shapes by name

observation_text: She spoke clearly: 'my brother is sleeping inside the room right now'.
Bina kisi atkan ke bolta hai: 'hume park chalna chahiye'.
He gave a detailed story about the dog chasing a cat up a tree.
School me drawing class me kya kiya pura bataya.
She said: 'tomorrow we will go swim in the pool'.
Kal hum nani ke ghar jayenge bolta hai.
He went to his room, got his bag, and waited at the gate when told.
Almari se kapde nikal kar bed par rakhne ka instruction follow kiya.
He laughed and closed his eyes playing peek-a-boo with his brother.
She rolled the toy ball back to me playing on the floor.
He hugged his mother when she sat next to him on the floor.
Mummy ko gale lagata hai jab wo office se aati hain.
I pointed to a plane, he looked at it, and then looked back at my eyes.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Looks at caregiver for joint attention

observation_text: Mera chehra dekhta hai jab hum chidiya ki taraf dekhte hain.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Looks at caregiver for joint attention

observation_text: He copies other kids running around the park playground.
Dusre bacche ko block stack karte dekh khud bhi waisa karne laga.
He showed sadness and offered his car to the crying baby.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Shows concern for a crying child

observation_text: Rote hue dost ko dekhkar usko apna khilona dene laga.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Shows concern for a crying child

observation_text: She looked at me to check reaction before touching the strange new toy.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Looks at parent face for reaction

observation_text: Mummy ki taraf dekha jab koi anjaan ghar aaya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Looks at parent face for reaction

observation_text: He played with block toys next to another child without talking.
Sandbox me dusre bacche ke paas khel raha tha bina baat kiye.
He got extremely angry and cried when he couldn't open the box.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Displays wide range of distinct emotions

observation_text: Khilona tootne par gusse me shor machane laga.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Displays wide range of distinct emotions

observation_text: She ran to greet her dad with a big smile when he walked in.
Dada ko dekhkar unke paas bhag gaya bina bole.
They took turns sliding down the park slide cooperatively.
Bacho ke sath milkar car se khel raha tha bari-bari.
He pretended to be a school teacher teaching his stuffed toys.
Doctor-doctor khel raha tha aur gudiya ki heartbeat check ki.
She hugged her mother and said 'do not be sad' when she cried.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Tries to comfort distressed family or friends

observation_text: Ghar me kisi ko rote dekh unko paas ja kar hug karta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Tries to comfort distressed family or friends

observation_text: He waited his turn while playing a board game with siblings.
Hide and seek khelne me rules follow kar raha tha.
She ran directly to the classroom at school waving goodbye.
Bina roye school ke gate ke andar chala gaya.
He said: 'superheroes are pretend, they cannot fly in real life'.
Usko pata hai ki kahani ka dragon sach me nahi hota.
He shook the metal keys to listen to the clinking sound.
She throws her spoon from high chair to watch it drop.
He placed five small blocks inside the plastic bucket.
Glass me chote toys daal raha tha.
She put the toy receiver to her ear pretending to talk.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Tries to use items for their intended purpose

observation_text: Kaan par phone lagakar babble karne laga.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Tries to use items for their intended purpose

observation_text: He sorted the plastic blocks into piles of red and green.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Sorts objects by shapes or colors

observation_text: Round rings ko round slot me daal kar match kiya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Sorts objects by shapes or colors

observation_text: He pressed piano keys to hear different sounds.
Pop-up toy ke button slide karke animal nikal raha tha.
He searched and found the car hidden under the sofa pillow.
Chupaya hua toy box pillow ke niche se nikal liya.
She pretended to feed her doll with a toy fork.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Engages in simple pretend play scenarios

observation_text: Dabbe ko gadi bana kar chala raha tha.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Engages in simple pretend play scenarios

observation_text: He matched the toy cat with the card showing a cat picture.
Ek jaise socks ko laundry basket me sath rakha.
He drew a rough circular loop on paper using a pencil.
Gol aakar gol-gol pen ghuma kar banaya.
He drew a circle for a head and two lines for legs.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Draws simple person with 2 to 4 parts

observation_text: Stick figure banai jisme sir aur do leg banaye.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Draws simple person with 2 to 4 parts

observation_text: She said: 'these two red balls are the same'.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Correctly matches same and different items

observation_text: Poocha ki inme se kaunsa block sabse alag hai to bataya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Correctly matches same and different items

observation_text: He said: 'it is night time, we must go to sleep now'.
Morning aur night ke concepts achhe se samajhta hai.
He drew his mother with a head, eyes, body, arms, and feet.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Draws person with at least 6 parts

observation_text: Drawing me sir, pet, dono haath aur ungliyan banai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Draws person with at least 6 parts

observation_text: She wrote numbers 1 to 5 on the workbook sheet.
Apna naam paper par pencil se likh diya.
He counted ten wooden pegs on the table, pointing to each one.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Counts ten or more objects correctly

observation_text: Ten blocks ko ungli rakh kar 1 to 10 gina.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Counts ten or more objects correctly

observation_text: He took ten steps independently walking across the carpet.
Bina kisi sahare ke kamre me chal rahi hai.
He pulled himself up using the coffee table to stand up.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Pulls self up to stand using support

observation_text: Sofa pakad kar khada ho gaya jab mai baithi thi.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Pulls self up to stand using support

observation_text: She climbed up onto the couch pillows on her own.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Climbs onto low furniture or steps

observation_text: Low child chair par aasani se chadh jata hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Climbs onto low furniture or steps

observation_text: He kicked the red ball forward in the backyard.
Ball ko kick kiya bina balance khoye.
He runs easily without falling down in the playground.
Park me bina thokar khaye tezi se daudta hai.
He climbed up three steps holding my hand.
Handrail pakad kar stairs chadhne laga hai.
He stood on the step and jumped off, landing on both feet.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Jumps off step with both feet

observation_text: Low bench se grass par dono pairon se kood gaya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Jumps off step with both feet

observation_text: He balanced on his right foot for two seconds.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Balances on one foot briefly for 2s

observation_text: Ek pair par do second tak stand kiya bina gire.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Balances on one foot briefly for 2s

observation_text: He pedaled the tricycle down the hallway.
Three-wheeler cycle ko pedals mar kar chalaya.
He hopped forward on one leg three times.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Hops on one foot a few times

observation_text: Ek pair par kood kar chalne ka practice karta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Hops on one foot a few times

observation_text: He caught the bounced ball using both hands.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Catches a bounced ball most of the time

observation_text: Bounce hone ke baad ball ko dono haathon se lapak liya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Catches a bounced ball most of the time

observation_text: He went up the stairs alternating left and right feet.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Climbs stairs alternating feet without support

observation_text: Adults ki tarah seedhiyon par bari-bari se pair rakh kar chadha.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Climbs stairs alternating feet without support

observation_text: He skipped down the sidewalk alternating feet smoothly.
Park me alternate hop kar ke skip kar raha tha.
She climbed across the monkey bars in the playground.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Swings and climbs on play structures

observation_text: Jungle gym aur monkey bars par bina help ke chadh gaya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Swings and climbs on play structures

observation_text: He stood on one leg and counted to ten without falling.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Stands on one foot for 10s or more

observation_text: Ek pair par das second tak khada ho gaya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Stands on one foot for 10s or more

observation_text: She pinched a piece of dry cereal using thumb and index finger.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses neat pincer grasp to pick items

observation_text: Ungli aur anguthe se chote daane utha leta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses neat pincer grasp to pick items

observation_text: He made scribbles on paper using a blue marker.
Crayon se paper par lakeerein banayi.
He turned the cardboard book pages one by one.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Turns pages of a book manually

observation_text: Kitab ke panne ek-ek karke palatta hai.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Turns pages of a book manually

observation_text: He built a tower of five wooden blocks.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Builds tower of at least 4 blocks

observation_text: Char blocks ki building banai jo nahi giri.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Builds tower of at least 4 blocks

observation_text: He scooped up applesauce using the spoon to eat.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses spoon to feed self partially

observation_text: Chammach se dahi khane ki koshish ki khana girne par bhi.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses spoon to feed self partially

observation_text: She opened the box lid to find the toy inside.
Gift box ka wrapping paper nikal diya.
He stacked eight blocks cleanly to build a high tower.
Aath blocks ek ke upar ek rakh kar tower banayi.
She snipped the edges of paper using safety scissors.
Safety scissors se paper katne laga.
He twisted off the cap of his water bottle.
Dadhkan ko ghumakar bottle khol di.
He cut paper along a straight line using safe scissors.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Cuts paper along straight line with scissors

observation_text: Paper ko seedha kaat kar do tukde kar diye.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Cuts paper along straight line with scissors

observation_text: He copied the capital letter H using a green crayon.
Alphabet T aur V copy karke paper par likha.
He buttoned two large buttons on his sweater on his own.
Shirt ke bade button khud se laga liye.
He drew a triangle shape with three distinct sides.
Tikona aakar paper par pencil se banaya.
He held the table knife to try cutting the banana slices.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses fork and table knife to eat

observation_text: Kante aur knife se khana khane ki koshish ki.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Uses fork and table knife to eat

observation_text: He successfully tied a single knot in the cotton string.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Tries to tie knots or thread laces

observation_text: Shoes ke lace holes me dori daalne ka try kiya.
ground_truth_milestone_title: Tries to tie knots or thread laces
